title: invos · Invoice Analytics
description: Business view of the ingested invoices (PostgreSQL). All panels query by invoice_date (event/simulated time), NOT created_at (ingest/processing time) — event-time analytics is a deliberate talking point. Default time range covers the generator's 90-day window starting 2025-01-01.

targets.rawSql: SELECT invoice_date AS "time", count(*) AS "invoice count", sum(total_amount) AS "revenue (NTD)" FROM invoices WHERE $__timeFilter(invoice_date) GROUP BY invoice_date ORDER BY invoice_date

options.content:
### What to look for

**Toothpaste by brand** (panel below) is the ad-campaign exhibit. The Step-2 generator runs a campaign for brand **PearlGuard** starting **simulated day 45 (2025-02-15)**: ~40% of households get a 1.5× toothpaste purchase lift and shift their brand preference toward PearlGuard.

Watch the **PearlGuard** line lift away from BrightMint/FreshWave after **2025-02-15**. Turn the campaign off in `generator/config.yaml` (`campaign.enabled: false`) and the lines stay tangled.

The exposed households and exact knobs are recorded in `generator/data/ground_truth.json`.

_All panels use `invoice_date` (event time), not `created_at` (ingest time)._

panels.title: Toothpaste daily quantity by brand (campaign lift after 2025-02-15)

targets.rawSql: SELECT i.invoice_date AS "time", sum(it.quantity) FILTER (WHERE it.brand = 'BrightMint') AS "BrightMint", sum(it.quantity) FILTER (WHERE it.brand = 'PearlGuard') AS "PearlGuard", sum(it.quantity) FILTER (WHERE it.brand = 'FreshWave') AS "FreshWave" FROM invoice_items it JOIN invoices i ON i.id = it.invoice_id WHERE it.category = 'toothpaste' AND $__timeFilter(i.invoice_date) GROUP BY i.invoice_date ORDER BY i.invoice_date

options.content:
#### Toothpaste brands

- **PearlGuard** — campaign brand
- BrightMint — control
- FreshWave — control

Before **2025-02-15** the three brands sit close together (per-household preference noise). After the campaign start day, PearlGuard's daily quantity pulls ahead as exposed households buy more toothpaste *and* skew toward PearlGuard.

Ground truth: `campaign.start_day = 45`, `lift = 1.5`, `exposed_fraction = 0.40`, `brand = PearlGuard` (see `ground_truth.json`).

targets.rawSql: SELECT it.category AS "category", sum(it.quantity) AS "quantity" FROM invoice_items it JOIN invoices i ON i.id = it.invoice_id WHERE $__timeFilter(i.invoice_date) GROUP BY it.category ORDER BY "quantity" DESC

panels.title: Average invoices per day-of-week (weekend lift)
panels.description: Mean invoices per calendar day, grouped by day of week. The generator's weekend_factor (1.25) makes Sat/Sun visibly taller than weekdays.

targets.rawSql: SELECT dow, "avg invoices/day" FROM (SELECT to_char(invoice_date, 'Dy') AS dow, EXTRACT(dow FROM invoice_date) AS ord, count(*)::numeric / count(DISTINCT invoice_date) AS "avg invoices/day" FROM invoices WHERE $__timeFilter(invoice_date) GROUP BY 1, 2) t ORDER BY ord
